results.title: AI Engineer at MSCI | Canary Wharfian
highlights:
# AI Engineer
...
**AI Engineer - Associate/Senior Associate | MSCI** **Key Responsibilities:** - Design, develop, and maintain AI-driven apps supporting MSCI's ESG, Index, Analytics, and Real Estate verticals. - Implement machine learning, NLP, and GenAI solutions, focusing on LLMs, RAG, summarization, and agentic AI systems. - Integrate AI services with MSCI's data science platforms, collaborating cross-functionally with tech, data, and engineering teams. - Ensure AI solutions comply with data governance, security, and responsible AI standards, and comply with regulatory requirements. **Required Skills & Experience:** - 4-7 years in AI engineering, data science, or applied machine learning. - Bachelor's or Master's degree in Computer Science, Mathematics, Engineering, or related field. - Strong foundation in machine learning, NLP, and statistical modeling. - Proven experience delivering AI/ML solutions into production using LLMs, LangChain, LangGraph, or similar. - Experience with software engineering best practices and CI/CD pipelines. **Preferred Skills:** - Cloud experience (Azure or GCP). - Familiarity with DevOps tools and CI/CD. - Understanding of data governance, responsible AI, or model risk management. - Prior experience in financial services or regulated industries.
...
We are seeking AI Engineers at Associate and Senior Associate levels to support MSCI's AI adoption journey, with a focus on machine learning, NLP, GenAI, LLMs, and agentic AI systems. These roles are hands-on and engineering-focused, centered on building, integrating, and operationalizing AI capabilities across internal platforms and client-facing products.

Implement machine learning, NLP, and GenAI solutions that move from experimentation to production.

...

results.title: Analyst, GP Solutions – Private Markets Product Strategy - Internexxus
highlights:
# Analyst, GP Solutions – Private Markets Product Strategy
...
### Job Description
...
Analyst, Private Assets Product Management | MSCI Inc.
...
The Tone: This is a full-time role at MSCI Inc. MSCI strengthens global markets by connecting participants across the financial ecosystem with research-based data, analytics, and indexes, supported by advanced technology. This role is crucial for building the data and AI infrastructure that the next generation of private markets will run on. Your contributions will genuinely influence how the industry evolves, with your thinking showing up in products used by leading private equity and credit managers worldwide.
...
The TL;DR • Role: Full Time • Type: Full-time • Pay: $80000–$105000 yearly • Team: GP Solutions team, focusing on general partners (private equity, growth equity, venture, and private credit managers). • Mission: Support the GP Solutions team in developing data- and AI-enabled product solutions for private markets by conducting research, aiding product strategy, and translating client workflows. • Tech Stack: Microsoft Office (Excel, PowerPoint, Word), data visualization tools, project management tools, Python, SQL, Tableau, Jira, Confluence
...
What You’ll Actually Do • Conduct market and competitive research to identify emerging trends and needs across GP workflows, synthesizing findings for product strategy discussions. • Support client discovery sessions by preparing materials, capturing insights, and distilling key takeaways from conversations with GPs and internal stakeholders. • Assist in developing product documentation, including user stories, requirements briefs, and feature specifications, to guide engineering and design teams. • Help track and prioritize incoming product requests from Client Coverage and Sales, supporting structured intake and business-as-usual governance processes. • Monitor post-launch product performance by gathering and organizing data on adoption, usage, and client feedback to inform future product iterations.
...
The Must-Haves • Background: Bachelor’s degree in finance, economics, business, computer science, or a related field. This is an entry-level role. • Experience: 0–2 years of relevant experience; internship experience in investment management, financial services, consulting, or technology is a plus. • Skills: Strong analytical and problem-solving skills, excellent written and verbal communication skills, high attention to detail and organizational skills, proficiency in Microsoft Office (Excel, PowerPoint, Word). • Bonus: Exposure to private equity, venture capital, private credit, or alternative investments; familiarity with product management concepts, agile methodologies, or software development workflows; experience with data analysis tools (e.g., Python, SQL, Tableau) or product management tools (e.g., Jira, Confluence); demonstrated interest in how technology and data can transform investment decision-making.
...

“Szilárd and me started working at Bosch on the same day, learning and growing together for almost 2 years. Already a skilled research engineer, his knowledge of machine learning, professionalism and wisdom brought great value to our team. When the time came to elect a new Product Owner, Szilárd acted as a real-life Jon Snow: he did not ask for the crown, but recognized (as everyone) that no one was a better fit to lead us, so he stepped up and became our PO. In this role he always acted responsibly and did his best to push our team goals, while keeping everyone happy. If anyone had questions or needed advice, be it research, engineering, professional or even unrelated to work, he was the person to go to, a voice of reason. Even though PO responsibilities easily consume full-time hours, he invested extra effort to keep his own goals going and develop as a research engineer. I am thankful for the time spent working next to him, and hope to do it some more in

results.title: Abhishek Avadhani
highlights:
Senior Associate (AI Engineer) at [MSCI Inc.](https://www.linkedin.com/company/msci-inc)
...
I’m a finicky software engineer and researcher obsessed with building complex and sometimes, AI-driven systems. I usually start by building something small… then add a few “supporting features”… then accidentally architect the whole system; and somehow, without my own fault, end up shipping a full product.I play with stacks like LangGraph, LangChain, OpenCV, TensorFlow, Keras, NumPy, Mediapipe, CUDA, Flask, FastAPI, Django, Tornado, NodeJS, Gin-Go, Dart, Flutter, ReactJS, and NextJS. I’ve architected end-to-end systems, built ML pipelines, and, out of sheer irritation, redesigned libraries just to make them behave.I’ve always had this itch to open things up -- machines, code, even feelings sometimes -- anything that hides its “how.” That curiosity started young, when I tried building a tiny arc reactor after watching Iron Man (it worked just long enough to shock me). Ever since, I’ve been driven by that same mix of curiosity, logic, and mild recklessness to understand how things work; and how to make them work better.Certified by HackerRank in Python, SQL, and REST APIs, with a B.E. in IT from Mumbai University (2020). Always tinkering, tweaking, and chasing the next “what if” in traditional computers and quantum computers.

results.title: MSCI names new CTO and opens Silicon Valley hub to advance AI product development
highlights:
MSCI names new CTO and opens Silicon Valley hub to advance AI product development
...
# MSCI names new CTO and opens Silicon Valley hub to advance AI product development
...
MSCI hired Kashi Kakarla as CTO and is opening a Silicon Valley hub to build AI into its indexes, risk tools, and portfolio analytics. Kakarla previously led AI product development at Intuit, including QuickBooks.
...
## MSCI Appoints New CTO and Opens Silicon Valley Hub to Build AI Into Core Products
...
MSCI has hired Kashi Kakarla as Chief Technology Officer and Head of Product Engineering, and is opening a new innovation hub in Silicon Valley. The moves signal a shift toward embedding AI more deeply into the indexes, portfolio tools, and risk analytics that asset managers and asset owners rely on daily.
...
Kakarla comes from Intuit, where he built cloud-based, AI-powered products for a large financial software user base. His experience turning QuickBooks into an AI-driven platform aligns with what institutional investors now expect from risk models and portfolio tools. He will report directly to the CEO and sit on the Management Committee, signaling that product engineering is no longer a support function but central to how MSCI competes.
...
The Silicon Valley hub gives MSCI access to engineering talent and proximity to companies like BlackRock, Bloomberg, and FactSet that are building decision-support tools for investors. For product teams, this means faster iteration cycles and the ability to test AI features against what competitors are shipping.
...
The real test is execution. Integrating AI into existing platforms is complex and time-consuming. If new features disrupt workflows for asset managers, client satisfaction could suffer. If done well, AI-powered research tools and portfolio analytics could increase subscription stickiness and deepen client relationships.
...
- Login to My Account
- How quickly MSCI announces AI-powered product updates in research tools, index analytics, and private markets data
- Client adoption rates and feedback on new features from asset managers and wealth managers
- Competitive positioning relative to S&P Global, Bloomberg, and FactSet, which are also investing in AI for investment tools
- References to the Silicon Valley hub on earnings calls and investor presentations
...
Product managers at investment firms should watch how MSCI's AI features integrate into their own workflows. The company's success here could shape what you expect from your data and analytics vendors over the next 18 months.
...
For those building products in this space, AI for Product Development covers how to evaluate and integrate AI into existing platforms without breaking what already works. The AI Learning Path for Product Managers walks through how to set roadmaps when AI features are part of the strategy.

results.title: VP, Artificial Intelligence - Products & Services | CareerSync
highlights:
# VP, Artificial Intelligence - Products & Services
...
About the Role & TeamIDC is seeking a transformative AI leader to define and execute our enterprise-wide AI strategy—embedding cutting-edge artificial intelligence across both internal operations and customer-facing products. This is a rare opportunity to shape how a global data and intelligence company harnesses AI, from agentic workflows and large language models to predictive analytics and AI-native product experiences. Reporting directly to the CTO, the VP of AI will lead a high-performing technical team, partner across business units, and drive measurable business impact through intelligent, responsible, and scalable AI solutions. The ideal candidate combines deep technical expertise in modern AI—including generative AI, LLMs, and agentic systems—with the strategic acumen to align AI investments to revenue growth and competitive differentiation. IDC sits at the intersection of proprietary data, expert human insight, and global technology reach—an ideal foundation for AI transformation. Key opportunity areas include: AI-augmented research & analyst productivity — deploying LLM-powered tools to accelerate data synthesis, report generation, and market forecasting. Intelligent data products — embedding predictive models and AI-driven insights directly into IDC's subscription and advisory offerings. Agentic content & marketing workflows — leveraging generative AI to personalize content, automate content pipelines, and optimize Foundry's MarTech capabilities. Enterprise AI infrastructure — building shared platforms, APIs, and MLOps practices that enable AI adoption at scale across all IDC business units. What You’ll Do
...
AI Strategy & Vision: Partner with the CEO, CTO, CIO, and business unit heads to define IDC's multi-year AI roadmap—spanning generative AI, agentic systems, predictive analytics, and AI-native product development. Technical Leadership: Architect and oversee IDC's AI platform and infrastructure, ensuring scalability, security, and alignment with enterprise data strategy. Product Delivery: Develop and execute IDC’s approach to agentic AI technology, thereby delivering the centerpiece of IDC Quanta, our cross-channel technology intelligence layer. Team Building: Recruit, develop, and retain a world-class AI team spanning ML engineers, data scientists, AI product managers, and MLOps specialists. Stakeholder Alignment: Translate complex AI capabilities into clear business value for executives and department heads; maintain transparent goals and resource planning. Ecosystem & Partnerships: Evaluate and manage AI technology partnerships, vendor relationships, and open-source contributions to accelerate IDC's AI capabilities. What You Bring
...
PhD. or Master's degree in Computer Science, AI/ML, Statistics, or a related quantitative field (or equivalent experience).
...
7+ years of experience in applied machine learning, AI engineering, or data science—with at least 3 years in senior technical leadership roles.
...
products and platforms that generated measurable business impact (revenue growth, cost reduction, or product differentiation).
...
Deep expertise in modern AI/ML and agentic/generative systems design.
...
Hands-on fluency with generative AI technologies: LLM fine-tuning, retrieval-augmented generation (RAG), prompt engineering, and agentic frameworks (e.g., LangChain, LlamaIndex, AutoGen).
...
Strong engineering foundation: Python, SQL, cloud ML platforms (AWS SageMaker, GCP Vertex AI, or Azure ML), and modern MLOps practices (CI/CD, model monitoring, feature stores).
...
Familiarity with knowledge graphs, semantic search, or AI applications in content and market research workflows.
...
Experience with responsible AI frameworks, model governance, and AI regulatory compliance (EU AI Act, NIST AI RMF).
...
Track record of building AI developer platforms or shared ML infrastructure used by multiple product teams.

results.title: vishnu B
highlights:
Data Analyst | ESG & Governance Data | AI-Powered Workflow Tools | Claude API · Power BI · SQL | MSCI
...
Operations Analyst at [MSCI Inc.](https://www.linkedin.com/company/msci-inc)
...
I'm a data analyst with 2+ years at MSCI, where I work across corporate governance, ESG, and financial data operations — spanning Controversies, Directors & Officers, and Vote Results teams. What sets me apart is that I don't just work with data — I build tools to improve how it's processed. I designed and built an AI-powered Vote Results Data Extractor using the Claude API and React, which automates structured data extraction from AGM documents across markets including the UK, India, Switzerland, and South Africa. The tool received strong endorsement from my team lead and is pending production deployment. I also built a companion Accuracy Validator with live dashboards, Excel export, and persistent storage. Earlier, I built the GICS Career Mapper — a GPT-based tool for the D&O team that extracts executive career details and maps them to GICS industry classifications, reducing manual effort and classification errors. My core skills span data validation, governance data workflows, escalation analysis, SQL, Power BI, Python, and prompt engineering. I'm currently based in Coimbatore and open to data analyst, governance analyst, and AI workflow roles in Coimbatore, Bangalore, or Chennai. Let's connect if you're working on data quality, ESG, financial integrity, or AI-powered automation.
...
Vote Results (VR) Team: 1. Supporting AGM (Annual General Meeting) document processing across multiple markets including UK, Netherlands, South Africa, India, Switzerland, and Italy. 1. Performing detailed data reviews to identify discrepancies, governance-related anomalies, and integrity issues, escalating complex cases with clear analytical justification. 1. Applying defined methodologies and controls consistently to support auditability, peer review, and downstream research and product usage. 1. Using Generative AI tools responsibly for data assessment and summarization, validating outputs through human review to ensure reliability and compliance. 1. Developed a production-ready AI-powered VR Data Extractor tool and companion Accuracy Validator (see Projects section), which received strong team lead endorsement pending deployment. Directors & Officers (D&O) Team: 1. Analyzed director and executive-level data, validating roles, career history, governance relevance, and entity associations. 1. Designed and built a custom GPT-based tool (GICS Career Mapper) to extract executive career details and map them to GICS industry and industry group classifications, reducing manual effort and minimizing classification errors. Controversies Team (News Clearing Process): 1. Reviewed and classified issuer-related news linked to ESG, integrity, and reputational issues. 1. Assessed events across defined severity levels (minor to non-minor) using established MSCI methodologies, ensuring accurate tagging, documentation, and escalation for auditability.

...
Extractor & Accuracy Validator
...
Built a production-ready AI-powered tool for MSCI's Vote Results team that automates structured data extraction from AGM (Annual General Meeting) documents across global markets including the UK, India, Switzerland, South Africa, Netherlands, and Italy. The tool accepts documents via text paste or PDF upload, calls the Claude API, and returns structured governance data across five sections: Company & AGM details, Proposals, Directors, Share Information, and TVO Calculator. Also built a companion Accuracy Validator with field-by-field validation, live accuracy dashboards, Excel export, and JSON backup — supporting a formal validation process before production deployment. # Data Scientist (100%)
...
## Drive innovation in machine learning and AI, from research to deployment, creating scalable intelligent systems that power the next generation of scientific discovery.
...
Are you passionate about turning complex data into intelligent, production-ready solutions while supporting the future of open-access science? We are looking for a Data Scientist to join our team and help design, develop, and deploy advanced machine learning solutions that power intelligent, data-driven products. This role focuses on NLP, recommender systems, and agentic LLM workflows, turning complex business challenges into scalable and impactful analytical solutions.
...
This is an opportunity to work at the forefront of machine learning and AI, contributing to innovative systems that leverage state-of-the-art research in practical applications.
...
Core Responsibilities
...
Design, develop, and evaluate machine learning and statistical models, with a focus on NLP, recommender systems, and LLM-based solutions.
...
Translate business problems into data-driven approaches and scalable analytical solutions.
...
Perform data exploration, preprocessing, and feature engineering to ensure high-quality inputs for modeling.
...
Develop, optimize, and benchmark models using appropriate metrics and validation strategies.
...
Build and experiment with LLM-powered systems, including agentic workflows and RAG.
...
Investigate, reproduce, and compare research prototypes and state-of-the-art methods.
...
Collaborate with engineering teams to support the integration of models into production.
...
